executiveSummary.text: Avacta is a clinical-stage oncology company built around its pre|CISION platform - a tumour-targeted prodrug activation technology designed to improve the therapeutic index of cancer drugs by releasing the active drug preferentially in the tumour microenvironment rather than systemically. The near-term catalyst is the Phase I data readout for farudodistat (AVA6000, a FAP-activated doxorubicin) expected in H1 2026, and two confirmed presentations at AACR (17-22 April 2026). A second pre|CISION pr

businessModel.text: Avacta's value is entirely in its pre|CISION platform and the clinical pipeline it generates. pre|CISION activates drugs specifically in the tumour microenvironment using Fibroblast Activation Protein (FAP), which is highly expressed in the stroma of most epithelial tumours. The FAP-activated prodrug approach is scientifically credible and has a mechanistic rationale that addresses a genuine clinical need - reducing the systemic toxicity of potent chemotherapies. FAP is expressed on cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs) across a broad range of solid tumours - not on the tumour cells themselves, but in the tumour stroma. This is the correct mechanistic interpretation: the pre|CISION platform targets the FAP enzymatic activity at the tumour-stroma interface. Primary literature supports FAP as a "promising pan-tumour target" with expression confirmed in multiple epithelial cancer types. [1,2] Exatecan - used as a pre|CISION payload in AVA6000's next generation - is a potent topoisomerase-1 (Topo-1) inhibitor with single-dose efficacy confirmed in primary literature. [3] The "most potent Topo-1 in clinical use" claim requires specific comparative data not yet confirmed in available literature; the general potency claim is well-supported. The commercial model will be either a partnership deal with a global pharmaceutical company (preferred, as it provides upfront cash and derisks development) or a US listing to access deeper capital markets. A NASDAQ dual listing or US partnership is the stated strategic direction and the £9.45M oversubscribed raise completed in early 2026 was structured to support this. The pipeline: farudodistat (AVA6000) is the most advanced pre|CISION asset - Phase I data expected H1 2026. AVA6200 (gemcitabine pre|CISION) is in IND/CTA-enabling studies targeting 2026 clinical entry. AVA7200 (immune checkpoint inhibitor pre|CISION) is in discovery. The business is essentially a platform validated by clinical data - each clinical readout de-risks or validates the platform thesis.

financialSnapshot.text: Market cap: £319.4M (live market data) Current price: 70.00p 52-week range: 26p - 84p Cash position: £9.45M oversubscribed raise completed early 2026; runway expected through key data readouts P/E: N/A - clinical-stage biotech EPS: -0.16 At the current market cap, Avacta is a higher-valued clinical-stage oncology name where the upcoming data still need to justify the valuation.

recentCatalysts.text: AACR Conference (17-22 April 2026): Two presentations confirmed - this is the primary near-term catalyst. The content of the presentations (early clinical data, biomarker data, safety profile) will drive sentiment significantly. AVA6000 Phase I data (H1 2026): This is the binary event. Phase I data showing preferential tumour activation with reduced systemic toxicity would validate the pre|CISION platform and trigger partnership/US listing discussions. Failure to differentiate from standard doxorubicin would be a significant setback. £9.45M oversubscribed raise (early 2026): Completed successfully - demonstrates institutional interest and provides runway through key clinical milestones. The raise was structured with a US placement component, consistent with the NASDAQ listing ambition. Partnership / NASDAQ dual listing: Both are actively being explored according to management. A US listing would dramatically improve liquidity and access to specialist oncology and biotech investors. A partnership deal would provide upfront cash and validate the platform clinically. AVA6200 IND/CTA filing (target 2026): Second pre|CISION programme entering the clinic would expand the pipeline and validate the platform beyond the initial doxorubicin programme.

thesisEvaluation.text: Bull case support (primary literature): FAP as pan-tumour target: FAP is confirmed expressed in the stroma of multiple epithelial cancer types. The platform's tumour-agnostic claim is mechanistically justified. [1,2] Cardiotoxicity TAM expansion: This is the strongest literature finding. DOX cardiotoxicity is mechanistically linked to FAP activation in cardiac fibroblasts - FAP PET signal is significantly elevated from 2 weeks post-DOX, versus 10 weeks for standard echocardiography detection. If pre|CISION reduces systemic DOX exposure, it could reduce this cardiotoxicity mechanism, enabling higher/frequent dosing. [4] Exatecan potency: A potent Topo-1 payload with confirmed single-agent efficacy and synergy with DDR inhibitors (ATR, PARP). [3] Bear case support (primary literature): FAP is a stromal, not tumour-cell target: The pre|CISION mechanism depends on stromal penetration and FAP activity at the tumour interface - this is scientifically sound but harder to optimise than direct tumour-cell targeting. Clinical validation is required. "Most potent Topo-1" claim: Specific comparative data (Exatecan vs Irinotecan/Topotecan in patients) not confirmed in available literature - this specific claim should be treated as unvalidated. Scenario Probability Price Target Key Drivers Bull 30% 150p+ Phase I data validates FAP tumour-targeting with reduced cardiotoxicity; partnership deal announced; US listing confirmed; platform expands to multiple programmes Base 40% 60-90p Phase I data is encouraging but not conclusive; more clinical work needed; partnership discussions continue but no deal yet; stock stays range-bound until next data catalyst Bear 30% 30-45p Phase I data disappoints; no platform differentiation from standard doxorubicin; partnership unlikely; NASDAQ listing delayed; funding becomes dilutive Weighted price target: 30% × 150 + 40% × 75 + 30% × 37.5 = 88.75p . Current price of 70.00p is below base-case fair value, but the risk remains high because the valuation still depends on clinical validation. Adjusted conviction: 63/100 . This remains a high-conviction speculative position - size accordingly.

keyRisks.text: 1 Phase I clinical data (binary): This is a binary event. The data for AVA6000 in H1 2026 - and the AACR presentations in April - will determine whether the pre|CISION platform actually works in humans. A negative read will cause severe re-pricing. 2 Partnership / NASDAQ timing: Both are strategically sound but timing is uncertain. A US listing at an inopportune moment (pre-data or post-disappointment) could be significantly dilutive or fail entirely. The strategic ambition requires clinical validation first. 3 Funding risk: £9.45M provides runway through key data readouts, but a negative Phase I outcome would require a rescue financing or very dilutive raise. Clinical biotech funding conditions remain difficult for unpartnered programmes. 4 FAP expression variability: The pre|CISION mechanism depends on consistent FAP expression in the tumour stroma across patient populations. Variability in FAP expression could limit the clinical utility of the approach in broader tumour types. [1,2] 5 DOX cardiotoxicity - mechanistic nuance: The primary literature confirms DOX cardiotoxicity runs through FAP activation in cardiac fibroblasts, which supports the pre|CISION thesis. However, this mechanistic link also means that incomplete FAP targeting (sub-therapeutic tumour delivery) could still produce cardiac effects while missing therapeutic benefit - a worst-of-both-worlds outcome. [4]

risks.risk: 1 Phase I clinical data (binary): This is a binary event. The data for AVA6000 in H1 2026 - and the AACR presentations in April - will determine whether the pre|CISION platform actually works in humans. A negative read will cause severe re-pricing.

2 Partnership / NASDAQ timing: Both are strategically sound but timing is uncertain. A US listing at an inopportune moment (pre-data or post-disappointment) could be significantly dilutive or fail entirely. The strategic ambition requires clinical validation first.

3 Funding risk: £9.45M provides runway through key data readouts, but a negative Phase I outcome would require a rescue financing or very dilutive raise. Clinical biotech funding conditions remain difficult for unpartnered programmes.

4 FAP expression variability: The pre|CISION mechanism depends on consistent FAP expression in the tumour stroma across patient populations. Variability in FAP expression could limit the clinical utility of the approach in broader tumour types. [1,2]

5 DOX cardiotoxicity - mechanistic nuance: The primary literature confirms DOX cardiotoxicity runs through FAP activation in cardiac fibroblasts, which supports the pre|CISION thesis. However, this mechanistic link also means that incomplete FAP targeting (sub-therapeutic tumour delivery) could still produce cardiac effects while missing therapeutic benefit - a worst-of-both-worlds outcome. [4]

whoShouldOwn.text: Should own: Specialist biotech investors who understand clinical binary risk and want genuine early-stage oncology platform exposure with a mechanistic thesis supported by primary literature. Position sizing is critical - this is a clinical data event name, not a steady hold. Size the position for the binary outcome. Should avoid: Anyone who cannot tolerate a 50-70% drawdown if the Phase I data disappoint. Investors who need operating revenue or a timeline to profitability. Anyone who cannot monitor the AACR catalyst in April 2026 closely.

recommendation.text: HOLD - SPECULATIVE at 70.00p. Hold through AACR (April 17-22), assess the data, and make a binary decision at that point. Do not add before the data without being prepared to act on the outcome. Primary literature conclusion: The mechanistic thesis - FAP targeting to reduce DOX cardiotoxicity while maintaining tumour efficacy - is mechanistically plausible and supported by peer-reviewed literature. The strongest bull case support comes from the FAP PET cardiotoxicity data: DOX activates cardiac FAP from 2 weeks post-treatment, before any functional cardiac change is detectable. This is a genuine clinical problem that pre|CISION could address. [4] AACR ALERT (17-22 April 2026): Two Avacta presentations are confirmed. Review the abstracts when published (usually AACR embargo lift) and the full presentations on-site or via webcast. Decision point: add on positive signal, reduce/hold on mixed data, exit on disappointment. Set a personal rule before the data arrives.

entryExit.text: AACR data (April 2026): Primary binary catalyst - full content review of both presentations required before any position change Pre-AACR (now to April 17): Hold - do not add materially without the data; 70.00p already reflects meaningful binary risk Post-AACR positive: Add - potential partnership or US listing news follow; re-evaluate on partnership deal terms Post-AACR mixed/negative: Reduce - Phase II path requires more data and additional capital; re-enter below 45p Conviction Trend Latest conviction: 63/100 .
